user.description: Select the Nuki Opener to set up.

choose_method.title: Connect to {name}
choose_method.description:
How do you want to authorize Home Assistant on {name} ({address})?

Pairing registers Home Assistant as a new (bridge-type) authorization. Choose manual entry only if you already have credentials, e.g. exported from nuki_hub or pyNukiBT.

pair.title: Pair with {name}
pair.description:
Put the Opener into pairing mode first: press and hold its button for 5 seconds until the LED ring lights up. Then press Submit.

Note: pairing must be enabled in the Nuki app (Settings → Features & configuration → Button & LED).

manual.title: Enter existing credentials
manual.description: All keys are hex encoded. The public key is derived from the private key automatically.

error.not_in_pairing_mode: The Opener is not in pairing mode. Press and hold its button for 5 seconds, then try again.
error.cannot_connect: Could not connect to the Opener. Check that a Bluetooth proxy or adapter is in range.
error.pairing_failed: Pairing failed unexpectedly. Check the logs for details.
error.invalid_credentials: The credentials are malformed. Check key lengths and encoding.

abort.already_configured: This Nuki Opener is already configured.
abort.not_supported: This device is not a Nuki Opener.
abort.no_devices_found: No Nuki Opener found. Make sure it is powered and a Bluetooth proxy or adapter is in range.

init.description: The security PIN (as set in the Nuki app) enables reading the activity log, which improves doorbell ring detection. Leave empty to disable.

data_description.lock_behavior: What unlocking the lock entity does. Buzzer mode fires the electric strike directly and relocks automatically — useful without ring-to-open wiring and for HomeKit.

error.cannot_connect: Could not connect to the Opener to verify the PIN.
